title: Shard the collection that you want to shard.

sections.heading: Required Roles
sections.body:
The following table describes the roles required to manage the
collections in an Atlas project:

| Action             | Required Roles                                                                                                        |
| ------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Create Collections | One of the following roles:Project Owner or Organization OwnerProject Data Access AdminProject Data Access Read/Write |
| View Collections   | At least the Project Data Access Read Only role.                                                                      |
| Drop Collections   | One of the following roles:Project OwnerProject Data Access Admin                                                     |
| Shard Collections  | One of the following roles:Project OwnerOrganization Owner                                                            |

sections.heading: Create a Collection
sections.body:
> **Tip:** To create the first collection in a new database, see
> [database names](atlas-ui-create-a-db).

> **Important:** You cannot create new collections on the `config` and
> `system` databases. Atlas will deprecate writing to existing
> collections on these databases in the near future.

To create a collection in an existing database through the Atlas UI:

- If it's not already displayed, select the
  organization that contains your project from the
  Organizations menu in the navigation bar.

- If it's not already displayed, select your project
  from the Projects menu in the navigation bar.

- In the sidebar, click Data Explorer under
  the Database heading.

  The [Data Explorer](https://cloud.mongodb.com/go?l=https%3A%2F%2Fcloud.mongodb.com%2Fv2%2F%3Cproject%3E%23%2Fmetrics%2FreplicaSet%2F%3Creplset%3E%2Fexplorer) displays.

Select or hover over the database, and click the  icon to
open the Create Collection dialog box.

In the Create Collection dialog box, enter the name of
the collection you want to create.

Atlas also provides Additional preferences. You can choose
from the following options:

- [Create a Clustered Collection](atlas-ui-clustered-collection)

- [Create a Collection with Collation](atlas-ui-collation-collection)

> **Important:** Don't include [sensitive information](sensitive-info) in
> your collection name.

For more information on MongoDB collection names, see
[Naming Restrictions](manual/reference/limits).

Select whether the collection is a
[time series collection](manual/core/timeseries-collections).
If you select to create a time series collection, specify the time
field and granularity. You can optionally specify the meta field and
the time for old data in the collection to expire.

Upon successful creation, the collection appears underneath the
database in the Connections sidebar.

sections.heading: View Collections
sections.body:
To view the databases and collections in the cluster through the
Atlas UI:

sections.heading: Step 1: View the collections in a database.
sections.body:
Click on the name of the database.

> **Note:** Atlas bases the document count that appears on this tab
> on cached metadata using [collStats](manual/reference/command/collStats). This count might differ from
> the actual document count in the collection. For example, an
> [unexpected shutdown](manual/reference/command/collStats) can throw off the
> count. Use the [db.collection.countDocuments()](manual/reference/method/db.collection.countDocuments) method for
> the most accurate document count.

sections.heading: Visualize Collection Data
sections.body: To launch MongoDB Charts to visualize data in your databases and collections.

sections.heading: Step 1: Launch .
sections.body:
To visualize data in MongoDB Charts from the Atlas UI, click
Visualize Your Data when viewing a specific database
or collection. Charts loads the data source and you can
start building a chart in the Charts view. For detailed
steps, see [Build Charts](charts/build-charts).

sections.heading: Drop a Collection
sections.body:
To drop a collection, including its documents and indexes, through the
Atlas UI:

In the Connections sidebar, click the 
icon next to the collection you want to drop. In the drop-down menu that
appears, click Drop Collection.

Confirm by typing the name of the collection, and click
Drop Database.

sections.heading: Shard a Collection
sections.body:
If you have large data sets and perform high throughput operations, you
can [shard](manual/sharding) a collection to distribute data across
the shards.

You cannot shard a collection through the Atlas UI. To shard a
collection, first confirm that your cluster is a [Deploy a Sharded Cluster](create-cluster-sharding), then take the following steps to shard the
collection in [mongosh](mongodb-shell):

See Connect to a Cluster via mongosh.

To create an index on the shard key, run the following command:

```sh
db.<collection-name>.createIndex({<shard_key_definition>})
```

> **Example:** To create an index on the shard key for the
> `sample_analytics.customers` collection:
>
> ```sh
> db.sample_analytics.createIndex( { "username": 1 } )
> ```

To learn  more, see:

- [Choose a Shard Key](manual/core/sharding-choose-a-shard-key)

- [createIndexes](manual/reference/command/createIndexes)

To shard a collection, run the following command:

```sh
sh.shardCollection("<database>.<collection>", { "<indexed-field>" : 1 } )
```

> **Example:** To shard the `sample_analytics.customers` collection:
>
> ```sh
> sh.shardCollection("sample_analytics.customers", { "username" : 1 } )
> ```

WARNING: If you shard a collection that already has a MongoDB Search
index, you might experience a brief period of incomplete search results
while the index is building on a shard. Also, if you add a shard for
an already sharded collection that contains a MongoDB Search index, your search
queries against that collection might return incomplete results until
the initial sync process completes on the added shards. To learn more,
see [initial sync process](troubleshoot-initial-sync).

To learn more, see [Shard a Collection](manual/tutorial/deploy-shard-cluster) in the MongoDB
manual.
